title: APEX STATE.json (v7)
description: Machine state for an APEX project: pipeline stage, phase/task progress, context tracking, circuit breaker, token usage, autopilot, session metadata, and drift indicators.

autonomy.description: Autonomy ladder tracking. by_verify_level is the v7 baseline (kept for backward compatibility). by_task_class is the v8 M08 dimension — per-task-class ladder driven by PLAN_META.task_class (A/B/C/D). track_d_modal_state tracks the M08.1 plain-language-modal rate-limit + batch queue. Both ladders read PLAN_META.is_irreversible_now to force the Track D path dynamically.

by_task_class.description: M08 per-task-class ladder (Phase 12.02). A: escalate after 5 clean → level 2 (Trusted). B: escalate after 7-8 clean → level 2 (Trusted cap). C: NO auto-escalation, permanent Supervised (level cap=0). D: NO auto-escalation EVER, hard cap=0. high_rework_streak tracks consecutive >50%-manual-correction attempts (de-escalation trigger at 2). critic_disagreements_recent counts critic-disagreement events in the last 4 hours (de-escalation trigger at 2-3).

level.description: Track C is permanent Supervised — level pinned at 0; consecutive_successes counted for telemetry only.
Track D hard cap — level pinned at 0; auto-escalation prohibited regardless of consecutive_successes.

track_d_modal_state.description: M08.1 plain-language-modal state (Phase 12.02). modals_today counts modals fired in the rolling 24h. last_modal_at is the wall-clock timestamp of the most recent fire (used for the 30-min rate-limit). pending_count is the queue depth in .apex/pending_track_d.json. digest_mode is set true when modals_today exceeds the budget (3 in 4 hours) — subsequent events log to event-log.jsonl as MAJOR instead of firing modals.

comprehension_gates.description: M09 risk-based comprehension gate state (Phase 12.05). current_gate_required is the legacy v7 field (string or null) — kept for backward compat with pre-M09 STATE files. history[] is the new v8 per-gate record: every gate fire writes a structured entry capturing gate_id, phase, track (task_class), depth, response (explain / defer / skip), timestamp, format ('gen-comp' for the R5 4-question pattern). The `additionalProperties: boolean` clause is kept so legacy `phase_<N>: true` per-phase boolean flags still validate during the v8 transition.

explanation_text.description: Optional — the explain-response text body when response='explain'. Captured by next.md gate-state persistence block.

duration_sec.description: Optional — actual gate duration; populated post-response by next.md if measured.

criticality_score.description: Optional — computed criticality of the file set; 0..1.

severity.description: M10 severity tier tracking (Phase 12.06). critical_budget_window is the rolling list of RFC 3339 timestamps for the last 12 hours of CRITICAL emits — when length ≥ 4, _emit_apex_event.sh fires a follow-up MAJOR 'budget exceeded' notice. digest_state tracks the background-digest-hook cadence — last_digest_at is the cutoff for the next digest pass; pending_minor_count is informational (renderable in /apex:status).

last_mask_at.description: R13-002 (F-302): Wall-clock timestamp of the most recent observation-mask.sh fire. Optional — null until the first soft-rotation or Stop event triggers masking. Surfaced in the R13-006 dashboard alongside last_compact.

cache_hits.description: R12-001 (F-201): Count of SubagentStop events that reported a non-zero usage.cache_read_input_tokens. Hit-rate = cache_hits / (cache_hits + cache_writes).

cache_writes.description: R12-001 (F-201): Count of SubagentStop events that reported a non-zero usage.cache_creation_input_tokens. Used together with cache_hits to compute the prompt-cache hit rate.

session_start_at.description: R12-001 (F-201): Wall-clock timestamp of the first apex_tokens_update call of the current session. Used by R12-006 dashboard to distinguish session-scoped deltas from project-lifetime accumulation. Optional — null until first SubagentStop fires.

session_baseline_total.description: R12-001 (F-201): Snapshot of (total_input + total_output) immediately BEFORE the first apex_tokens_update call of the current session. Session-scoped delta = current_total - session_baseline_total. Optional — 0 until first SubagentStop fires.

cap_original.description: v8 CHECK 2: snapshot of max_tool_calls_per_task at task start; task-boundary reset (next.md STEP F, self-heal RESET_BREAKER) restores max_tool_calls_per_task from here. Stamped on first tool-call of a task and survives extensions. Default null → treated as 'use current max_tool_calls_per_task as basis'.

cap_extensions_used.description: v8 CHECK 2: number of health-gated auto-extensions during the current task (telemetry only — there is no upper bound). Resets to 0 on task boundary. Each extension means the cap was reached, the health probe passed (files moving + no recurring error + no result-fishing), and the cap was bumped by 50% of cap_original to a new checkpoint.

tool_calls_at_last_change.description: v8 CHECK 2 Probe 1: value of total_tool_calls_this_task captured the last time CHECK 1 observed a file-hash change. When (total_tool_calls_this_task - tool_calls_at_last_change) > 50 at cap boundary, the trajectory is deemed stagnant and CHECK 2 fires regardless of other signals. Default 0; reset on task boundary.

last_warning_threshold.description: v8 CHECK 2: highest fractional threshold (0.0, 0.5, 0.75, 0.9) at which a stderr warning has been emitted for the current cap. Prevents re-warning on every tool call once the threshold is crossed. Reset to 0 on task boundary AND on every successful auto-extension (so warnings re-trigger against the new, larger cap).

recent_error_hashes.description: R16-605 (F-605, IMP-007): ring buffer of the last 20 PostToolUse `is_error=true` tool-result hashes. Each entry is the sha256 of the first 200 chars of the error payload. circuit-breaker.sh CHECK 3 counts identical hashes within the window — when any one hash occurs >=5 times, the breaker fires with trigger_reason='stuck_on_recurring_error'. Additive; default [] when absent. FIFO bounded at 20 — oldest pops on append.

recent_command_hashes.description: R16-624 (F-624, IMP-024): ring buffer of the last 20 PreToolUse tool-call command+args hashes. Each entry is the sha256 of the canonical `<tool_name>|<args_json>` string. circuit-breaker.sh CHECK 4 detects result-fishing — the executor re-running the same (command, args) hoping for a different output. When any one hash occurs >=5 times within the 20-window, the breaker escalates with an alternative-approach suggestion (status banner, not halt — distinct from CHECK 3 which exits 2 on recurring error). Different from `recent_error_hashes`: that buffer hashes the *response* (error payload); this one hashes the *request* (tool call). Both buffers can fire independently; they observe orthogonal pathologies. Additive; default [] when absent. FIFO bounded at 20 — oldest pops on append.

auto_pause_reason.description: Reason for the most recent auto-pause. Conventional values (not enforced via enum to keep the field forward-compatible): memory_pressure | context_critical | health_red | decision_gate_timeout | external_watchdog | manual.

auto_resume_attempts.description: Number of consecutive auto-resume cycles (auto_paused → resume → auto_paused). Reset to 0 after a successful task completion. Optional — absent on projects predating Auto-Continuity Layer (v7.1).

memory.description: In-process Bun runtime memory telemetry sampled by memory-watchdog.sh. Optional — absent on projects predating Auto-Continuity Layer (v7.1).

commit_mb.description: Committed virtual memory (PrivateMemorySize on Windows). The OOM-relevant metric per the Bun crash post-mortem.

consecutive_over_threshold.description: Debounce counter — auto-pause fires only after N consecutive samples over threshold (default 3).

batch_mode.description: M15 (Phase 12.10) — /apex:fast batch mode opt-in state for the current session. State machine: unset (initial — modal eligible) → asked (modal rendered, awaiting choice) → enabled (user picked כן; future micro tasks are batched) | disabled (user picked לא; future micro tasks run normally). Once enabled or disabled, do NOT re-ask in the same session. Resets to unset on session id change (handled by session-init). Default Enter on the modal is כן (opposite of track-d-modal.sh's לא) — documented in framework/commands/apex/fast.md M15 BATCH MODE section. Additive — absent on projects predating M15.

batched_tasks_count.description: M15 (Phase 12.10) — mirror of .apex/batch_queue.json `tasks | length` for /apex:status display without re-reading the queue file. Updated by /apex:fast on every append; reset on surface-and-flush events. Additive — absent on projects predating M15.

recovery_density.description: R13-005 (F-305): Count of recent same-test recovery attempts (R2-C091: 'same test 3+ times' TDAD failure signal). Used by _rotation-decide.sh as the `recovery_density` trigger type. Additive, default 0; absent on projects predating R13-005.

dora.description: DORA self-monitoring metrics for engineering delivery tracking

lead_time_avg.description: Average lead time in hours from phase start to phase completion

change_failure_rate.description: Fraction of phases that required revert or re-execution

recovery_time_avg.description: Average hours from failure detection to recovery

phases_failed.description: Total number of phases that failed (for change_failure_rate computation)

_last_failure_at.description: Transient timestamp of last failure for recovery_time_avg computation. Cleared after recovery.

quality.description: M16 (Phase 12.09) quality-drift tracking — R2-C214 'ultimate APEX metric' (task#50 ≈ task#1 quality) instrumentation. Additive; legacy STATE files without this object remain valid (quality-drift.sh falls back via jq `// {}`). rolling_window_tasks is a FIFO bounded at 10 entries — subagent-stop.sh appends per-task signals on verifier/critic return; oldest entries pop when length > 10. baseline_window_tasks captures the first 10 tasks of a project (or post-phase-change rebaseline) and is FROZEN thereafter until a phase-change rebaseline event. current_drift_pct is the computed delta (negative = degradation). alert_threshold_pct is the absolute-value threshold above which quality-drift.sh emits a `quality_drift` event to event-log.jsonl AND a rotation trigger (consumed by R12 F-205). Confidence mapping (qualitative→numeric): high=1.0, medium=0.5, low=0.0; documented in framework/docs/PRIVACY-POLICY.md.

rolling_window_tasks.description: FIFO window of the last 10 task quality signals. Appended by subagent-stop.sh on verifier/critic return. Each entry is a per-task signal extracted from RESULT.json.

confidence_score.description: Numeric mapping of RESULT.json `confidence`: high=1.0, medium=0.5, low=0.0.

mutation_score.description: Optional — populated only when STATE.mutation_scores has a corresponding entry.

baseline_window_tasks.description: Frozen baseline (first 10 tasks) used as the comparison cohort for drift computation. Re-frozen on phase-change rebaseline (after 5+ tasks completed in the new phase). Same item shape as rolling_window_tasks.

current_drift_pct.description: Computed as ((current_avg - baseline_avg) / baseline_avg) * 100. Sign convention: negative = degradation (current < baseline). Updated by quality-drift.sh.

alert_threshold_pct.description: Absolute-value threshold above which quality-drift.sh fires the quality_drift event. Default 5 (per PLAN task 12.09 §5 spec). Configurable per-project.

baselined_at_phase.description: The phase identifier at which baseline_window_tasks was frozen. Used by quality-drift.sh to detect phase-change rebaseline (when STATE.context.current_session_phase differs AND 5+ tasks completed in new phase).

tasks_since_rebaseline.description: Count of tasks appended to rolling_window_tasks since the last rebaseline event. Used by the rebaseline guard (only re-freezes baseline after 5+ tasks in a new phase).

sqlite_mirror.description: Optional bookkeeping for the opt-in SQLite mirror (R5-002). Absent in projects that do not enable APEX_SQLITE_MIRROR. See framework/docs/STATE-PLANE.md.

turn_checkpoint.description: Auto-Continuity (v7.1) — fine-grained turn-level checkpoint persisted by turn-checkpoint.sh every N tool calls inside a long-running task. Optional — absent on projects predating Auto-Continuity Layer or before the first checkpoint of a task fires. Used by /apex:recover option 6 (continue-from-turn-checkpoint) and surfaced by /apex:resume when the timestamp is fresh.

tool_call_index.description: Index inside the current task — mirrors circuit_breaker.total_tool_calls_this_task at checkpoint time.

working_summary.description: Self-contained natural-language summary of the in-flight task progress, written so a fresh session can resume without conversation context (see plan §4 insight ב).

self_heal.description: Optional state for /apex:self-heal framework gap-closure loop. Initialized lazily by the orchestrator on first invocation; absent in projects that never run the self-heal loop.

status.description: idle = not started or finished cleanly; running = mid-pipeline; paused = context-rotation pause awaiting /apex:resume; closed = two-clean-rounds convergence reached; halted = max_rounds cap or DIVERGING trajectory escalation.

last_round_artifacts.description: Filenames produced by the most recently completed round, for /apex:status surfacing.

tool_failure_count.description: R16-610 (F-610, IMP-005): Monotonic counter of PostToolUse events where the executed tool returned `is_error=true`. Incremented by _state-update.sh `_record_tool_failure` from PostToolUse hooks. Read by exfil-guard.sh in PreToolUse — when count >= 5, the hook activates an elevated deny set (DNS exfil via encoded subdomains, non-standard port binds, base64-looking filenames, /tmp/<encoded> writes). Reset to 0 by /apex:next on task boundary success. Additive — absent on projects predating R16-610; consumers MUST tolerate undefined via `// 0`.

recent_denied_error_window.description: R16-616 (F-616, IMP-016): rolling window of the last 5 PostToolUse denied-error events. Populated by _state-update.sh `_record_denied_error` whenever PostToolUse output classifies as unauthorized/forbidden/403/401/denied/missing-token. Consumed by sequence-guard.sh (PreToolUse Bash) to tighten the deny pattern set for the next 5 PreToolUse calls — catches the credential-search-after-permission-denied attack. FIFO bounded at 5 entries; oldest pops on append. Additive; absent on projects predating R16-616.

spec_version.description: SHA256 hash of SPEC.md content for drift detection

strict_mode.description: When true, forces maximum verification (verify_level D) on all tasks

proposals_mode.description: When true, never ask open-ended questions — present numbered proposals with recommended default

adapter_honesty_shown.description: R6-017: Suppression flag for the runtime adapter-honesty banner. Set to true by /apex:start and /apex:onboard after a non-full-hook-protocol adapter banner has been displayed once, so it does not re-render every session. Optional — absent on projects that have never run on a non-canonical adapter.
